title: BeliefMatrix
description: Per-character four-layer belief model. Layer 1 is the ground truth (objective facts). Layer 2 is what the character THINKS is true (including their own misperceptions). Layer 3 is autobiographical memory with emotional weight and distortion. Layer 4 is hidden secrets the character holds but other characters (and the player) may not know about. The Resolver is the only writer; NPCs and the Director read it via the memory-recall service which filters by scene context.

characterId.description: Character this belief matrix belongs to. One matrix per character per run.

runId.description: Run this snapshot is bound to. Optional because the schema is also used as a template.

description.description: Plain prose statement of the objective fact. Authoritative — written by Resolver only.

establishedAt.description: Event sequence number at which the fact was canonized.

establishedBy.description: Resolver policy ID or narrative-contract beat that canonized the fact.

isContested.description: True iff at least one character holds a different belief_state for this fact.

objective_facts.description: Layer 1: ground truth, shared across all characters.

subject.description: Fact-ID or character-ID or concept this belief is about.

belief_state.description: How the character relates to the subject. 'wrong' = believes the opposite or a falsehood; 'denied' = actively refuses to accept; 'reinforced' = freshly re-confirmed this turn.

confidence.description: Subjective confidence. Clamped to [0,1]; 'wrong' or 'denied' usually correlates with high confidence in the wrong answer.

reasoning.description: In-character explanation for why the character holds this belief.

evidenceMemoryIds.description: Memory IDs the character uses to justify this belief.

character_knowledge.description: Layer 2: per-character cognitive map. May diverge from objective_facts (this is the source of drama).

emotional_weight.description: How strongly this memory is charged. High-weight memories are vivid and likely to fire.

distortion_type.description: How the memory has been bent by the character. 'confabulation' = LLM-fabricated detail to fill gaps; Resolver will likely demote these.

recallCount.description: How many times this memory has been recalled. Used for memory decay.

decayScore.description: 1.0 = fresh, 0.0 = forgotten. Decay accelerates for low-emotional-weight memories.

character_memories.description: Layer 3: autobiographical memory. Emotional weight + distortion type drive the memory-recall agent.

content.description: What the secret actually is. May or may not be objectively true (some secrets are delusions).

isSecret.description: If true, the player does NOT know this. If false, the secret has been revealed and exists in the public event log.

knownByCharacterIds.description: Character IDs aware of the secret. Resolver uses this to prevent leaks to non-aware characters.

leakageRisk.description: Probability the secret is accidentally surfaced this turn. Clamped to [0,1].

hidden_secrets.description: Layer 4: hidden information. Player and Resolver-only; NPCs cannot read each other's secrets.
